---
title: "Singapore Birth Rate EDA"
author: "Kiran Shankaran"
date: "2026-03-24"
output: html_document
---

## 1. Introduction

GitHub Repository:
https://github.com/kiranshank12519/Time-Series-Project-Singapore-Births-Forecast/tree/main

This report presents an exploratory data analysis (EDA) of Singapore's 
Total Live Births (TLB) and Total Fertility Rate (TFR) from 1960 to 2024.
The aim is to identify the temporal features of both series, assess 
stationarity, and propose candidate time series models for the period 
1960 to 2012, which will later be used to forecast TLB and TFR from 
2013 to 2024.Singapore has experienced a dramatic and sustained decline in 
fertility since the 1960s, influenced by government population policies, 
urbanisation,rising education levels, and economic development. These contextual 
factors will be referenced throughout the analysis.

---

## 2. Data Loading and Preparation

The data was sourced from the Singapore Department of Statistics via 
data.gov.sg. It contains 65 annual observations from 1960 to 2024 for 
two variables: Total Live Births (TLB) and Total Fertility Rate (TFR). 
No missing values were present in the dataset.
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load the data
births <- read.csv("singapore_births.csv")

# Rename columns
colnames(births) <- c("Year", "TFR", "TLB")

# Reverse so years go 1960 to 2024
births <- births[nrow(births):1, ]
rownames(births) <- NULL

# Check it looks right
head(births)
tail(births)

# Convert to time series objects
TLB <- ts(births$TLB, start = 1960, frequency = 1)
TFR <- ts(births$TFR, start = 1960, frequency = 1)

# Training period 1960-2012 only
TLB_train <- window(TLB, start = 1960, end = 2012)
TFR_train <- window(TFR, start = 1960, end = 2012)

```
